matches.match_rationale: The verification gap is fundamentally a broken workflow: alumni self-report, nobody follows up, employers are never contacted. A task automation agent can orchestrate the multi-step process — triggering employer outreach emails at set intervals post-graduation, logging responses, escalating non-responses, and updating a verification status record — closing the loop that currently never closes. This is exactly the 'multi-step approval/confirmation workflow' use case the capability is built for.
matches.opportunity_sketch: An agent that fires at 90-days post-graduation: sends employer verification requests, tracks responses, flags unverified claims as 'unsubstantiated' in the outcomes database, and compiles accreditation-ready substantiation reports on demand.

matches.match_rationale: A research agent can autonomously cross-check alumni self-reported data against public sources — LinkedIn profiles, company websites, professional registries — without requiring employer cooperation. This provides a scalable spot-check mechanism that doesn't depend on alumni or employer responsiveness, giving accreditation bodies independently gathered corroboration. For a 500-staff operator graduating hundreds of students annually, manual spot-checks are infeasible; autonomous research agents make it tractable.
matches.opportunity_sketch: A research agent that samples a random cohort of alumni claims each quarter, searches public professional profiles, and produces a corroboration confidence report flagging discrepancies — ready for accreditation submissions as third-party-style evidence.

matches.match_rationale: Self-reported alumni salary and role claims follow predictable distributions for a given program type — statistical outliers are likely misrepresentations or errors. Anomaly detection can flag claims that deviate significantly from cohort norms (e.g., reported salary 3x the cohort median, claimed title implausible for a recent graduate) for targeted manual verification, making the spot-check process systematic rather than arbitrary.
matches.opportunity_sketch: A model trained on historical verified outcomes that scores each new self-report for plausibility, routing high-anomaly cases to a human reviewer before claims are accepted into the official outcomes database.

matches.match_rationale: Two distinct generation needs exist here: (1) drafting personalized employer verification outreach emails at scale, calibrated by industry and relationship type, and (2) generating accreditation-ready outcome substantiation narratives that weave verified data points into compliant report language. Both require producing professional prose from structured data inputs — a core text generation task.
matches.opportunity_sketch: A text generation layer that takes a structured verification dataset and produces accreditation submission narratives, and separately generates batched employer outreach emails personalized to company type and program — reducing staff time from hours to minutes per accreditation cycle.

matches.match_rationale: Alumni outcome data arrives in incompatible formats: survey form responses, email replies from employers, LinkedIn exports, phone interview notes. Before any verification or reporting can happen, this fragmented data must be normalized into a unified schema (graduate ID, reported role, reported salary, verification status, source, date). Data transformation AI handles the messy ETL that makes systematic accountability possible in the first place.
matches.opportunity_sketch: A transformation pipeline that ingests alumni survey exports, employer email replies, and LinkedIn data extracts — mapping each to a canonical verification schema — feeding a central outcomes database that can be queried for accreditation reports.

matches.match_rationale: Full outcome verification is inherently a multi-stage pipeline requiring specialized agents working in sequence: a research agent gathers public profile data, an anomaly detection agent flags suspicious claims, a generation agent drafts employer outreach, and a transformation agent normalizes responses into the outcomes database. No single agent capability handles the end-to-end flow; orchestrating them as a multi-agent system delivers a complete accountability infrastructure.
matches.opportunity_sketch: An orchestrated pipeline — research → anomaly flag → outreach generation → response normalization → report generation — that runs each graduating cohort through a systematic verification lifecycle, producing accreditation-ready substantiation as a by-product.
